scope: Pure domain invariants: reachability, owner-scoped regions, check-add-create, wrappers, multi-tree consistency.

tests.name: integration: register two users, build a multi-tree graph, exercise all four reads
integration: §15.6 envelope codes (400, 403, 404) round-trip through the new routes
integration: Phase-1 notFound fallback still applies to unknown route paths
integration: POST /reset wipes BOTH the registry and the taxon store in one call
invariant 1: a linear chain has no cycles
invariant 1: a cross-tree diamond has no cycle
invariant 1: a self-edge A→A is a cycle that names A
invariant 1: a two-cycle A→B→A reports both taxa
invariant 1: a three-cycle reachable from a root chain is reported as a cycle
invariant 1: a cyclic graph does NOT cause invariant 2 or 3 to also fire spuriously
invariant 2: a linear chain passes
invariant 2: a cross-tree diamond passes (diamonds across trees are permitted)
invariant 2: an in-tree diamond reports the duplicated taxon with its root
invariant 2: a deeper in-tree diamond is still caught
invariant 2: shared-across-trees AND duplicated-within-one fires only for the in-tree case
invariant 2: two independent in-tree diamonds in different trees both fire
invariant 3: a tree with distinct names passes
invariant 3: two taxa with the same exact name in the same tree clash
invariant 3: case-variant names collide (§3.4 case-insensitive)
invariant 3: same name across different trees is fine (per-tree only)
invariant 3: a shared taxon clashing in one of its containing trees fires for that tree
invariant 3: rename simulation — a name that clashes in another containing tree is caught
evaluateAll: a graph violating only invariant 1 reports cycle, not 2 or 3
evaluateAll: a graph violating only invariant 2 reports duplicate, not 1 or 3
evaluateAll: a graph violating only invariant 3 reports name_clash, not 1 or 2
evaluateAll: a graph violating BOTH 2 and 3 reports both, attributed correctly
evaluateAll: an empty graph is ok:true
evaluateAll: a non-trivial clean multi-tree graph is ok:true
reachability: empty graph has no roots
reachability: a single isolated taxon is its own root
reachability: in a chain A→B→C, only A is a root
reachability: cross-tree diamond (A→C, B→C) has roots [A, B]
reachability: detach makes a previously-child taxon a root again
reachability: in chain A→B→C, every taxon's containing tree is {A}
reachability: cross-tree diamond — C's containing trees are {A, B}
reachability: deeper multi-root graph — D in two trees, B in one
reachability: a taxon with no parents is its own containing tree
reachability: treesContaining on an unknown id returns []
reachability: an isolated root is not shared
reachability: a linear-chain taxon is not shared
reachability: a taxon reachable from two distinct roots is shared
reachability: in-tree diamond does NOT make a taxon shared (single root)
reachability: a clean tree reports no in-tree duplicates
reachability: in-tree diamond reports the duplicated taxon
reachability: cross-tree diamond is NOT an in-tree duplicate in either tree
reachability: visit counts pin path-count semantics on a diamond
reachability: descendants of a leaf is empty
reachability: descendants from the root covers the whole tree
reachability: descendants from mid-tree is the strict downward closure
reachability: descendants is cycle-safe
checkAddCreate
safe create passes
sibling name clash in target tree is detected
case-variant sibling name clash is detected
create whose payload-parent is shared clashes in OTHER tree
create under nonexistent parent is structurally fine (no §3.3 violation)
create alone cannot introduce a cycle
wrapper does not mutate g.taxa
wrapper does not mutate g.childrenOf
wrapper does not mutate g.parentsOf
violations on clash carry the minted id
repeat calls with same input return deterministic order
delegates to checkInvariants in source
Invariant 1 — no cycles
empty graph passes
a tree passes
across-tree diamond passes (not a cycle)
self-loop is detected
two-taxon cycle is detected
three-taxon cycle is detected
disjoint cycles emit at least two violations
Invariant 2 — per-tree uniqueness of occurrence
across-tree sharing is legal
in-tree diamond is detected
deeper in-tree diamond is detected
duplication in two separate trees emits two violations
across-tree share with in-tree diamond in only one tree is reported once
Invariant 3 — per-tree name uniqueness (case-insensitive)
different names pass
same name in different trees passes
exact-case duplicate in one tree is detected
case-variant duplicate in one tree is detected
cross-tree same-name with in-tree clash in one tree only flags that tree
checkInvariants composite
returns ok on a valid graph
aggregates cycles before name clashes (documented order)
includes all three violation kinds when each is violated
purity
checkers do not mutate input
checkers do not throw on pathological graphs
ownerScopedRegion: basic cases
leaf-only region (n is a leaf owned by U)
single-child wholly-owned region
wholly-owned subtree (textbook case)
ownerScopedRegion: halt-frontier behavior
halts at other-owned child and records the edge
cascade does NOT continue past halt frontier even into owner-owned descendants
multiple halt-frontier children produce multiple frontier entries
halt-frontier child reachable via a separate wholly-owned path stays out of region; other route still flagged
ownerScopedRegion: case-insensitivity and edge cases
case-insensitive owner matching
unknown n yields empty region
other-owned n yields empty region
cyclic graph terminates and includes nodes once
ownerScopedRegion: purity
does not mutate the input Graph
roots(g)
empty graph has no roots
a parentless taxon is a root
multiple parentless taxa are all roots
a child taxon is not a root
containingRoots(g, t)
a root returns itself
a leaf traces up to its single root
a shared taxon returns multiple roots
sharing via different ancestor chains returns all containing roots
isShared(g, t)
is true iff containingRoots size > 1
roots are never shared
reachableSubtree(g, top)
includes only top for a leaf
includes top and all descendants
descends only from the requested top
terminates on a cyclic graph
terminates on a self-loop
purity
reachability functions do not mutate the input graph
reachability functions do not throw on cyclic graphs
checkAddEdge
safe add passes
cycle-creating add is rejected
self-loop add is rejected
in-tree diamond add is rejected
in-tree name-clash add is rejected (case-variant)
idempotent add (already-existing edge) passes
does NOT mutate input graph
checkRename
rename to same name is ok (no self-clash)
rename to an unused name passes
rename causing same-tree clash is rejected
rename causing case-variant clash is rejected
rename causing clash in a DIFFERENT containing tree is rejected
cross-tree rename with no clash anywhere passes
does NOT mutate input graph
ignores taxon-name-format validation (the wrapper's concern is §3.3 only)
